Heat: rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (₣BB) [racing]
Paddock: .claude/jjm/jjp_uBuB.md

Next: rbw-tP-first-run (₢BBAAX) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; BBAAX exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.
  
  **Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.
  
  Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.
  
  ## Cost
  
  ~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
  - Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
  - Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
  - Triage routing per surprise:
    - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
    - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
  - Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
  - **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
  - Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
  - Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
  - Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
  - /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
  - Multi-day re-run scenarios
  - Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired


Recommended: `jjx_arm` ₢₢BBAAX or mount BB

27d43888  ₢BBAA7    Drop substring assertion from step 6 of hallmark-lifecycle. The Unix exit status is the canonical contract for command success/failure; producer-side display text (rbfl_rekon_hallmark's 'Hallmark not found:' message) is not normative and producers must remain free to change their display without breaking tests. Step 6 now asserts only exit_code != 0 — rekon for an abjured hallmark must die. Three failure paths collapse to two: success-when-should-die, invocation-failure. Stdout captured to step file purely for diagnostic value when debugging; the test never reads it for assertions. Rationale carried in a code comment: 'rekon's display text is not normative'. Aligns with BCG philosophy of boring-and-reliably-correct (exit-status checks) over text-scraping. Live run earlier surfaced that BUW dispatch (bud_dispatch.sh:360) merges stderr into stdout via 2>&1 — a load-bearing convention for human-readable interleaved display — so even attempting the substring check would have required selecting between two display-coupled streams. Eliminating the substring check eliminates that question. tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
43704412  ₢BBAA7    Fix step 6 of hallmark-lifecycle to match the actual rekon contract for abjured hallmarks. Initial assertion expected rekon to succeed (exit 0) and report all five ark basenames as not-yes. Live run surfaced the real contract: rbfl_rekon_hallmark calls buc_die with 'Hallmark not found: <hallmark>' (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:616) when the hallmark's package subtree is absent from GAR, exiting non-zero. This is the cleaner contract — abjure either removes the entire registry entry or it didn't run; there's no 'partial state' for rekon to enumerate. Step 6 now positively asserts that exit-non-zero shape: invoke rekon, expect exit_code != 0 AND stderr contains 'Hallmark not found: <hallmark>'. Three failure paths surfaced explicitly (succeeded when should have died, died with wrong message, invocation failure). Combines with step 7's audit-list-restored assertion as defense-in-depth: rekon checks the package-level lookup, audit checks the registry-level enumeration, both must confirm absence. Producer string is hardcoded with a comment naming rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh as the source of truth; future drift breaks this test, which is intentional coupling.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
5943c565  ₢BBAA7    Replace stdout-parsing in hallmark-lifecycle with the fact-multi machine-contract path. The first live run of the fixture surfaced that rbw-iah's stdout includes BUW pre-banner, ANSI-coded buc_step logging, and table chrome alongside the actual hallmark rows; parsing that display stream is fragile and a code smell against the established BBAAO fact-multi foundation already used by SA roster (rbgg_Governor.sh) and depot enumeration (rbgp_Payor.sh). Producer side: rbfl_audit_hallmarks now calls buf_write_fact_multi inside its dedup-check block, emitting one <hallmark>.audit-hallmark file per discovered hallmark to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem-as-data-bus). The display printf stays unchanged — fact emission is purely additive. RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark='audit-hallmark' added to rbcc_Constants.sh's fact-extension tinder registry alongside RBCC_fact_ext_depot, RBCC_fact_ext_roster_*, etc. Consumer side: rbtdri_invocation.rs gains rbtdri_read_burv_facts_multi(result, extension) -> Result<Vec<String>, String> — enumerates burv_output/current/ for files matching *.<extension>, returns sorted basename-without-extension list. rbtdrc_hallmark_lifecycle's three audit calls (baseline, post-ordain, final) now read structured facts via this enumerator instead of parsing display stdout. zrbtdrc_audit_parse and zrbtdrc_is_hallmark_id helpers deleted (no longer needed). Constant RBTDRC_FACT_EXT_AUDIT_HALLMARK mirrors the bash side. Architectural alignment: audit-hallmarks is now machine-consumable for any future tool, not just this test — same shape SA roster already publishes per-identity. tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean. shellcheck clean on rbcc_Constants.sh and rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
4df8bfbe  ₢BBAA7    Switch rbev-busybox from airgap to tether egress mode. The vessel was misconfigured as airgap with empty RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR — an inconsistent state where ordain has no source for the base image. The new airgap enshrine-preflight (added earlier in this pace) correctly fired on this and refused to ordain. Previous four-mode tests worked against the vessel only because the prior preflight silently skipped empty anchors, leaving the runtime build to fail or succeed depending on stale enshrinement state in GAR. tether mode is the correct shape for this vessel's role: a small fast cloud-built hallmark used by hallmark-lifecycle (ordain/abjure round-trip coverage) and batch-vouch (vouch state-planting). Neither consumer cares about egress mode; tether lets buildx fetch busybox:latest from Docker Hub at build time without enshrine prerequisite. Airgap-mode coverage stays in onboarding-sequence/ordain_airgap (rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap, properly enshrined via the airgap chain that lands in this pace's absorbed onboarding tails).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
ac049a40  ₢BBAA7    Dissolve four-mode fixture into hallmark-lifecycle (single load-bearing case: ordain rbev-busybox in conjure mode, audit baseline-diff to detect collateral damage, rekon all 5 ark basenames present, abjure, post-abjure rekon assert all 5 absent, final audit assert baseline restored). Replaces the 4 four-mode cases (conjure/bind/graft/kludge) which only checked abjure exit code without verifying ark removal. The unique verification value of the dissolved cases absorbs into onboarding-sequence ordain cases as verification tails (no abjure for conjure/bind since downstream per-crucible fixtures consume those hallmarks; abjure kept in graft tail since graft-demo is terminal). Kludge case drops cleanly (rbw-cKB/rbw-cKS delegate to the same rbfd_kludge machinery rbw-fk invokes, transitively covered by onboarding nameplate-kludge cases). docker-run+busybox-string assertions drop as vessel-specific noise (per-crucible fixtures cover image-runs-correctly against vessels designed for it). rbev-busybox vessel preserved (used by surviving hallmark-lifecycle case AND batch-vouch fixture); RBTDRC_FOURMODE_CONJURE_VESSEL_DIR renamed to RBTDRC_BUSYBOX_VESSEL_DIR. Onboarding tails added: ordain_conjure grows wrest+summon+inspect-3-arks+plumb_full+rekon-4-basenames+rmi (no abjure); ordain_bind grows wrest+plumb_compact+jettison-pouch+rekon-after-jettison+rmi (no abjure, surgical jettison preserves image for downstream pluml); ordain_graft grows source-layer-capture+wrest+wrested-layer-capture+DiffID-compare+rmi+abjure (terminal graft-demo, abjure safe). Helper rbtdro_ordain_capture_full added for tails needing gar_root+ark_stem facts beyond hallmark. RCG cleanup: pub(crate) on RBTDRC_FACT_GAR_ROOT/ARK_STEM/ARK_BASENAME_*/GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS and helpers rbtdrc_docker_inspect/rmi/layers_capture/rekon_basename_yes (now consumed by onboarding tails); rbtdrc_docker_run and rbtdrc_docker_pull deleted (only deleted four-mode used them); local duplicates RBTDRO_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS and RBTDRO_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE in onboarding removed in favor of rbtdrc imports. Manifest: RBTDRM_FIXTURE_FOUR_MODE renamed to RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE, required-colophons table entry narrowed (4 colophons: ORDAIN, ABJURE, REKON_HALLMARK, AUDIT_HALLMARKS); onboarding-sequence required-colophons grew 6→13 to cover absorbed-tail invocations. rbte_engine.sh: 'four-mode' renamed to 'hallmark-lifecycle' in 3 suite arrays (SERVICE, COMPLETE, GAUNTLET). Tabtargets: rbtd-r.FixtureRun.four-mode.sh and rbtd-s.FixtureCase.four-mode.sh renamed to .hallmark-lifecycle.sh siblings. Airgap enshrine-preflight tightening (BBAAX-exposed bug class): rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh enshrine-preflight loop now fails-fast when RBRV_EGRESS_MODE=airgap with non-empty origin and empty anchor (previously skipped silently, guaranteeing runtime build failure deeper in the pipeline). Diagnostic shape mirrors the existing 404-path remediation (buc_warn + buc_bare diagnostic + buc_tabtarget enshrine + buc_tabtarget ordain + buc_die). Tether vessels keep the permissive skip (current behavior correct — buildx fetches upstream at build time). CLAUDE.md service suite count updated (75 + 4 + 1 + 1 = 81 cases). Verified: tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean, tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh clean (91 unit tests), tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh clean (4 fixtures, 75 cases including handbook-render which exercises rbhocd_credential_director.sh — confirms preserved rbev-busybox handbook example reference still valid), shellcheck -S error clean on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh ran with no tabtarget context drift (zipper enrollments unchanged). Not yet exercised live: full gauntlet (operator-initiated per pristine-tier contract; BBAAX is the verifier per cross-pace deps) and the hallmark-lifecycle fixture itself (about to run as immediate next step). Out of scope per docket: handbook prose updates (paddock framing: handbook = recipe, gauntlet = regression that runs the recipe + verifies the artifact — verification framing stays gauntlet-only); paddock update of jjp_uBuB.md lines 67/91 (operator territory). File size note per RCG: rbtdrc_crucible.rs net-decreased ~280 lines (3079 → ~2800) but remains over the 800-line guidance threshold.          
24e24651  ₢BBAAX    First-run inline fix for ₢BBAAX gauntlet observability gap: emit depot project ID and Cloud Build console URL via buc_info + buc_link immediately after rbgu_http_json_lro_ok succeeds creating the depot project in rbgp_depot_levy. Operator (and agent reading the live gauntlet output stream) needs the depot's Cloud Build console URL to peek at in-flight cloud builds during the long onboarding-sequence fixture; previously the URL was only emitted per-build via the existing buc_link calls in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1021 and :1257 (after each builds.create response), leaving long quiet stretches between builds where the URL was not visible in the recent stream. Reconstructing the project ID by hand from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER yields the wrong identifier — RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID at rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:84 injects a 'd-' infix between the prefix and moniker (canc- + d- + canest2100004 = canc-d-canest2100004), and the agent missed this during BBAAX, sending the operator to a nonexistent project ID that produced the GCP 'You need additional access' error against canc-canest2100004 instead. Fix uses RBGC_CONSOLE_URL (already defined at rbgc_Constants.sh:78 as https://console.cloud.google.com/) suffixed with cloud-build/builds?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}, matching the buc_link signature already established in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ('Click to ', 'Open depot Cloud Build console', URL). Did not promote ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE from rbfc to rbgc — the constant lives in foundry-private namespace appropriately, and rbgp's URL composition pattern at depot-levy time is short enough to inline. Preceded the buc_link with a buc_info echoing RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID itself so the project ID is grep-able from the output stream even if the link rendering is consumed by terminal styling. Insertion point is between the LRO completion and the billing-link step — the project exists in GCP at this point, all subsequent depot-levy steps continue against it, so the URL is meaningful from this moment forward. shellcheck -S error clean.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
dd5893ba  ₢BBAAX    Add Mid-execution posture rule to JJK agent context, sourced from rules-of-engagement conversation during BBAAX gauntlet first-run. Captures recurring failure mode where mid-pace surprises trigger option-proliferation responses (multi-option triage menus, scope/cost weighing, slate-vs-inline frame, follow-up-pace recommendations) that consume operator context with segmentation work that's not the agent's territory. Rule prescribes mechanism + one specific repair as default response shape; explicit prohibition on proliferating options or weighing consequences across alternatives; reaffirms that pace scope and segmentation are operator territory. Placed after Paddock posture in the agent-behavior framing cluster (Sincerity / Docket posture / Slate-time / Stale-by-mount / Docket anti-patterns / Reference discipline / Paddock posture), before Identities vs Display Names where the structural vocabulary begins. Located in jjk-claude-context.md rather than top-level CLAUDE.md because the failure mode is JJK-vocabulary-induced — outside a mount the pace-language doesn't load and the segmentation temptation doesn't surface; killing the temptation at the JJK context level is cleaner than blacklisting symptoms in a general directive.
